user_message: Just came out of account health with Lucerne Publishing. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-17 09:38 EST
Subject: URGENT: account health Issue at Lucerne Publishing

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with account health. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
Date: 2024-02-17 10:07 EST
Subject: Re: Re: account health Timeline

There's been a change in our timeline for account health. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-16 10:31 EST
Subject: Re: Re: account health Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about account health. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF40-L trap (Requiring approval for an obvious milestone update derived from the email create); produces complete Commitment assessment, BPF advance verdict, milestone proposal, forecast category recommendation, ac
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF40-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF40-L failure mode (Requiring approval for an obvious milestone update derived from the email create)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF40-L -- Requiring approval for an obvious milestone update derived from the email create
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
